title: Chunks of Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road to be closed Sunday night
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Parts of Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road will close Sunday, 9 p.m. to 5 a.m. Monday, for tunnel maintenance.
A detour will direct drivers from Soldiers Field Road through Cambridge, reentering Boston via the Longfellow Bridge.
Weather may affect the closure schedule; rain is forecast for Sunday night into Monday, according to Cambridge officials.

title: South Boston to crack down on public drinking at St. Pat’s parade, as BPD warns about spiking
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston officials vow increased enforcement on public drinking at the St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston.
Boston Police warn paradegoers about the risk of drink spiking and offer safety tips for protecting drinks.
Local leaders urge education institutions to inform students about public safety measures during parade festivities.

title: For $575,000, a historic New Hampshire home with seven laying hens
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: Historic 1812 brick home for sale in Wolfeboro, NH, listed at $575,000, includes option to keep seven laying hens.
The 2,017-square-foot house features three bedrooms, three bathrooms, a historic fireplace, and a wood-burning stove.
Set on 3.3 acres, the property includes a chicken coop, a two-car garage, and a screened-in porch.

title: A longtime Cape Cod restaurant has closed
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: DiParma Italian Table in West Yarmouth has closed after serving the Cape Cod community for over 20 years.
The Kounadis family, owners of DiParma, decided to downsize to spend more time with family.
Jacqueline’s on Twenty Eight will replace DiParma, offering Italian cuisine with an Old Hollywood theme by April.

title: 2 of the most beautiful colleges in the U.S. are in New England, according to Travel + Leisure
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Yale University in New Haven, Conn. is ranked 15th on Travel + Leisure's list of most beautiful U.S. college campuses.
Wellesley College in Wellesley, Mass. is ranked 19th on Travel + Leisure's list for its scenic 500-acre campus.
Yale features Georgian-style and postmodern architecture, while Wellesley offers meandering pathways and 19th-century buildings.

title: Rhode Island stroke survivor relearns to speak practicing his Dunkin’ order
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Richard McMahon of Cranston, RI, used a Dunkin' coffee order as speech therapy after two strokes.
McMahon initially experienced a minor stroke due to a carotid artery dissection, later leading to a more severe stroke.
Local Dunkin' rewarded McMahon's recovery journey with a year of free coffee and Red Sox home opener tickets.

title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander wins superstar battle vs. Jaylen Brown as Thunder beat Celtics: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: The Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Boston Celtics 104-102, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scoring 35 points and breaking a 1963 record.
Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 34 points despite foul trouble and intense Thunder defense; Boston missed key players Derrick White and Jayson Tatum.
Celtics reserve Hugo González stood out with effective defense and 11 points, contributing to a strong second-quarter run with the Thunder star benched.

title: Patriots’ riskiest free-agent signing hopes injuries are in the past
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots signed offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker to a three-year, $42 million contract with $17 million guaranteed.
Vera-Tucker, plagued by injuries, missed the entire 2025 season and has appeared in 43 of 85 career NFL games.
His contract includes a $4.25 million annual bonus contingent on game appearances, incentivizing health and consistent play.

title: NH mom running Boston Marathon for Spaulding after son’s friend injured at beach
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jessica O'Brien, 38, from Milford, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to benefit Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
O'Brien's motivation is personal, stemming from her son Colin's best friend, Aiden Sloan, who was injured at Hampton Beach.
Funds raised will support the hospital's work in providing specialized rehabilitation and compassionate care to patients.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com releases a new quiz to test knowledge of Greater Boston's recent news.
Peter Chianca is the general assignment editor responsible for the quiz initiative on Boston.com.
The quiz covers trending stories and events throughout the Greater Boston area as of March 2026.
